contract.title: Will TikTok be banned in the United States by April 30, 2025?
contract.resolution_criteria:
Resolves YES if, on or before 2025-04-30, TikTok is unavailable in the United States
via the Apple App Store or the Google Play Store, OR a federal directive (PAFACA
enforcement order from the Department of Commerce / Department of Justice) takes
effect requiring a divestiture or shutdown. Resolves NO otherwise.

settlement_entities.name: Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States

conditions.label: TikTok absent in US Apple App Store
conditions.summary: On or before 2025-04-30, TikTok is not available to US users in the Apple App Store (unlisted/blocked in the US storefront).

conditions.label: TikTok absent in US Google Play
conditions.summary: On or before 2025-04-30, TikTok is not available to US users in the Google Play Store (unlisted/blocked in the US storefront).

conditions.label: Federal PAFACA order takes effect
conditions.summary: On or before 2025-04-30, a DOC or DOJ PAFACA enforcement order takes effect requiring TikTok/ByteDance to divest or shut down US operations.

contract.narrative: Regulatory noise surfaced but lacked bite: a CFPB privacy speech and FTC cooperation MOUs signaled general scrutiny, while state insurance and disciplinary bulletins were unrelated and produced no PAFACA/DOJ action or app-store removals. Consequently, no nationwide TikTok ban occurred by April 30, 2025, and the contract resolved No.

timeline.title: D25-03 - Steven House - Amended Stipulated Agreement and Order
timeline.regulator: Alaska Department of Commerce, Community and Economic Development, Division of Insurance — Division of Insurance
timeline.one_line_why: State insurance agreement (Alaska Dept. of Commerce Division of Insurance) unrelated to TikTok, app stores, or federal PAFACA/DOJ actions.

timeline.title: Regulatory Board Disciplinary Actions Archive
timeline.regulator: Tennessee Department of Commerce and Insurance
timeline.one_line_why: State disciplinary archive about local Tennessee businesses unrelated to TikTok, app stores, ByteDance, or federal PAFACA actions.

timeline.title: Bulletin B25-05
timeline.regulator: Department of Commerce, Community, and Economic Development, Division of Insurance — Division of Insurance
timeline.one_line_why: Alaska Division of Insurance bulletin concerns state insurance/IRO matters and has no bearing on TikTok app availability or federal PAFACA enforcement.

timeline.title: Prepared Opening Remarks of CFPB Director Rohit Chopra at the Aspen Institute on Abuse and Misuse of Our Personal Data
timeline.one_line_why: CFPB remarks increase general scrutiny of data-harvesting apps but give no direct authority or new action that would remove TikTok or trigger a PAFACA order.
timeline.effective_date: CFPB will soon propose a rule

timeline.title: Cooperation Agreements
timeline.regulator: Federal Trade Commission — Bureau of Consumer Protection
timeline.one_line_why: FTC cooperation agreements are not direct causes of App Store/Play removals or a PAFACA order; at best a minimal indirect signal of scrutiny.
timeline.effective_date: Effective upon the effective date of the Open Internet Order

primary_drivers: Recent amended stipulated agreement and order (D25-03 - Steven House) driving enforcement attention
Regulatory board disciplinary actions archive highlighting multiple enforcement cases
Policy bulletins (B25-05) and CFPB remarks signaling supervisory and cooperation focus
heat_panel.explainer: Heat is driven by a recent amended stipulated agreement and concentrated attention to disciplinary records and regulatory bulletins, despite week-over-week cooling.
